subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Fever vs Hyperthermia
description: A short lesson to help you apply Fever vs Hyperthermia.

hook.text: A fever and heatstroke both raise body temperature. But they are opposite problems.

buildup.text: Fever is deliberate—the hypothalamus raises its set point to fight infection with heat.

discovery.text: Hyperthermia is a failure—the body gains heat faster than it can shed it. The thermostat didn't change.

twist.text: Fever rarely exceeds 104°F because the thermostat controls it. Hyperthermia has no upper limit.

climax.text: Treating them requires opposite approaches: fever responds to medication, hyperthermia needs cooling.

punchline.text: Fever fights infection. Hyperthermia is cooling failure. Opposite wars.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between fever and hyperthermia?
options: Fever is a controlled immune response; hyperthermia is cooling system failure
They are the same condition with different names
